revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hōjō Tokiyuki]][[es:Hōjō Tokiyuki]][[fa:هوجو توکی‌یوکی]][[fr:Hōjō Tokiyuki]][[id:Hōjō Tokiyuki]][[ja:北条時行]][[th:โฮโจ โทกิยูกิ]][[uk:Ходзьо Токіюкі]][[vi:Hōjō Tokiyuki]][[zh:北條時行]]
{{Short description|Japanese samurai}}
{{dablink|For the Hiroshima High School principal and Scouting notable, see [[Hōjō Tokiyuki (Scouting)]].}}
{{family name hatnote|Hōjō|lang=Japanese}}
{{nihongo|'''Hōjō Tokiyuki'''|北条 時行||died 21 June 1353}} was a samurai of the {{ill|Hōjō clan|en|Hōjō clan|qid=Q834364|lt=Hōjō clan|1=Hōjō clan}} who fought both for and against the Imperial Court.{{efn|Unlike every other source consulted, for example Goble,{{sfn|Goble|1996|p=246}} on page 38 of his "History of Japan", British historian {{ill|George Sansom|en|George Sansom|qid=Q349345|lt=George Sansom|1=George Sansom}} states that Tokiyuki was killed on 8 September 1335, by Ashikaga forces entering Kamakura.}} His father was {{ill|Hōjō Takatoki|en|Hōjō Takatoki|qid=Q2355334|lt=Hōjō Takatoki|1=Hōjō Takatoki}}, a {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|qid=Q1045904|lt=Shogunal Regent|1=Shikken}} and ''de facto'' ruler of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|qid=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}.

== Biography ==

Tokiyuki had fought against both the Imperial forces and those of the Ashikaga in order to save the Kamakura shogunate, of which his clan had been the ''de facto'' ruler for over a century. After the 1333 {{ill|Siege of Kamakura (1333)|en|Siege of Kamakura (1333)|qid=Q7510143|lt=siege of Kamakura|1=Siege of Kamakura (1333)}}, his father's suicide and the almost complete destruction of his family, he escaped to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|qid=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} and the home of [[Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]], where he gathered an army with which to return and try to regain power. He re-entered {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}} in 1335, forcing {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|qid=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi|1=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}} to flee before he was forced to flee himself by Tadayoshi's elder brother and future ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|qid=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}}'' {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|qid=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}.<ref name=Sansom2>{{Cite book |last=Sansom |first=George |title=A History of Japan, 1334–1615 |url=https://archive.org/details/historyofjapan00sans |url-access=registration |publisher=Stanford University Press |year=1961 |isbn=0804705259 |page=[https://archive.org/details/historyofjapan00sans/page/37 37]–38}}</ref> 

Shortly after his defeat, Tokiyuki asked to be pardoned by {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|qid=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, and formally entered into the service of the [[Southern Court]] (then rival of {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|en|Ashikaga shogunate|qid=Q1154406|lt=Ashikaga shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate}} and {{ill|Northern Court|en|Northern Court|qid=Q1201819|lt=Northern Court|1=Northern Court}}), fighting under the command of {{ill|Kitabatake Akiie|en|Kitabatake Akiie|qid=Q1036864|lt=Kitabatake Akiie|1=Kitabatake Akiie}}, and later {{ill|Prince Munenaga|en|Prince Munenaga|qid=Q541057|lt=Prince Munenaga|1=Prince Munenaga}}. He also aided in the 1352 recapture of Kamakura, led by {{ill|Nitta Yoshioki|en|Nitta Yoshioki|qid=Q3342234|lt=Nitta Yoshioki|1=Nitta Yoshioki}}. However, when routed Nitta was pursued by Ashikaga Takauji and sought refuge in {{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|qid=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province|1=Echigo Province}}, Tokiyuki fled to {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|qid=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province|1=Sagami Province}}, where he was discovered and beheaded by forces loyal to the Ashikaga.

== In popular culture ==
Hōjō Tokiyuki is the protagonist of ''{{ill|The Elusive Samurai|en|The Elusive Samurai|qid=Q105100468|lt=The Elusive Samurai|1=The Elusive Samurai}}'', a manga series that started in January 2021. He is portrayed as a young boy who is talented in fleeing and hiding.

== See also ==
*{{ill|Yokoi Yayū|en|Yokoi Yayū|qid=Q3572466|lt=Yokoi Yayū|1=Yokoi Yayū}}. The Yokoi clan is believed to descend from Hōjō Tokiyuki.
*''{{ill|The Elusive Samurai|en|The Elusive Samurai|qid=Q105100468|lt=The Elusive Samurai|1=The Elusive Samurai}}'' - A {{ill|Manga|en|Manga|qid=Q8274|lt=Manga|1=Manga}} series loosely based on the tales of Hōjō Tokiyuki.
*{{ill|Later Hōjō clan|en|Later Hōjō clan|qid=Q431928|lt=Later Hōjō clan|1=Later Hōjō clan}}

== Further reading==

{{citation needed|Hojo, Tokiyuki}}
